common_offers.notes: Low barrier but no commitment — attracts tire-kickers
Price-driven — our offer competes on value stack, not price

winning_ads.creative_description: Video of coach celebrating a member hitting a personal record on the treadmill. Upbeat music, member is sweating and smiling. Caption reads 'This is what 45 minutes looks like.' Running 12+ days.
winning_ads.hook_text: This is what 45 minutes looks like.
winning_ads.why_it_works: Celebrates outcome (joy/achievement) not effort. Social proof embedded in the creative. First-class-free removes all risk.

winning_ads.creative_description: Carousel of 5 real member photos — no models. Each slide shows a different body type. Final slide: 'Pilates is for every body.' Running 9 days.
winning_ads.why_it_works: Inclusivity angle differentiates from performance-focused competitors. Real members = trust signal.

new_this_week.hook_text: No commitment. No judgment. Just results.
new_this_week.notes: Added after spring motivation push — testing judgment-free angle for late April

disappeared_this_week.likely_reason: Seasonal — new year angle no longer resonates in late April

recommendation_for_ahri: Avoid all generic seasonal hooks ('get fit this summer', 'join now and save') — market is saturated. Lead with the coached experience angle and specific outcome stories. The Orangetheory video celebrating a PR is the strongest ad in market right now — AHRI should test a similar format showing a real member moment, not a gym tour. The No-Risk Comeback offer is structurally differentiated from all competitor offers — lean into the '1 dollar to start' and 'coached first month' specifics in all creative.
